patterns: Show me what you can do
responses: I can answer university FAQs, discuss NLP concepts, help with general questions, and have casual conversations!
I'm capable of: answering university queries, explaining NLP concepts, and general chat. Try me!
My skills include: understanding natural language, answering FAQs, explaining AI/ML concepts, and more!

patterns: Tell me about natural language processing
responses: NLP stands for Natural Language Processing! It's a branch of AI that helps computers understand, interpret, and generate human language.
Natural Language Processing (NLP) is the field of AI focused on the interaction between computers and human language. It enables machines to read, understand, and derive meaning from text.
NLP combines linguistics and machine learning to allow computers to process, analyze, and understand human language in a useful way.
Tokenization is the process of breaking text into individual units called tokens. For example, 'I love NLP' becomes ['I', 'love', 'NLP']. It's the first step in most NLP pipelines!
Tokenization splits text into smaller chunks (tokens). These can be words, sentences, or even characters. NLTK's word_tokenize() is a popular tool for this!
In NLP, tokenization means dividing a sentence into individual words or phrases (tokens). Example: 'Hello world' → ['Hello', 'world']. It's fundamental to text processing.

patterns: What is the difference between stemming and lemmatization
responses: Lemmatization reduces words to their base/dictionary form (lemma). For example, 'running' → 'run', 'better' → 'good'. Unlike stemming, it considers the word's context and part of speech!
Lemmatization converts words to their root form using vocabulary and morphological analysis. 'Caring' → 'care', 'Studies' → 'study'. NLTK's WordNetLemmatizer is widely used for this.
Lemmatization is smarter than stemming! It maps words to their meaningful base form using a dictionary. Example: 'wolves' → 'wolf', 'was' → 'be'.
Stopwords are common words like 'the', 'is', 'at', 'which' that appear frequently but carry little meaning. Removing them helps NLP models focus on important words!
Stopword removal is the process of filtering out high-frequency, low-meaning words from text. Examples: 'and', 'the', 'is', 'in'. NLTK has a built-in stopwords corpus!
Stopwords are words that don't contribute much to the overall meaning of text. By removing them, we reduce noise and improve model accuracy. Common stopwords: 'a', 'the', 'is', 'are'.

patterns: What is term frequency inverse document frequency
responses: TF-IDF stands for Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency. It measures how important a word is in a document relative to a collection of documents. Common words get lower scores!
TF-IDF is a numerical statistic used to reflect word importance. TF = how often a word appears in a doc. IDF = how rare the word is across all docs. High TF-IDF = important word!
TF-IDF is a feature extraction technique that converts text into numerical vectors. Words frequent in one document but rare overall get higher scores, making them more distinguishable.
Intent recognition is the process of identifying what a user wants to accomplish from their message. For example, 'Book a flight' has the intent 'book_flight'. It's the core of chatbot intelligence!
Intent classification uses machine learning to categorize user inputs into predefined intents. Our bot uses Logistic Regression with TF-IDF features to classify your messages!
In NLP, intent recognition means understanding the purpose behind a user's message. A model is trained on labeled examples to predict which category (intent) a new message belongs to.

patterns: How do I apply for admission
When is the deadline for admission
What documents are needed for admission
responses: For university admission, you typically need to: 1) Submit an application form online, 2) Provide academic transcripts, 3) Submit a motivation letter, 4) Pass an entrance test if required. Check your university's official portal for specific deadlines!
Admission requirements generally include: academic records, identification documents, recommendation letters, and test scores. Visit the university admissions office or website for the exact process.
The admission process varies by university. Generally: fill online application → submit required documents → await verification → receive admission letter. Contact the registrar's office for details!

Universities typically offer programs in: Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, Medicine, Law, Social Sciences, Arts, and more! Check your university's academic catalog for the complete list.
Common university departments include: Science & Technology, Business, Humanities, Medical Sciences, Engineering, and Education. Each department offers multiple degree programs!
Most universities offer Bachelors, Masters, and PhD programs across various disciplines. Popular programs include CS, MBA, Engineering, and Data Science. Check the university website for details!

patterns: What is the cost of education
How much is the semester fee
responses: Tuition fees vary by program and institution. Typically, science and engineering programs cost more than arts programs. Contact the university's finance department for the exact fee structure!
Fee structures depend on the program, nationality (local/international), and mode of study. Most universities offer payment plans and scholarships. Check the finance office for details!
University fees include tuition, exam fees, lab fees, and miscellaneous charges. Many students qualify for financial aid or scholarships. Visit the university's financial services page!

patterns: How can I get a scholarship
responses: Many universities offer merit-based, need-based, and research scholarships! Merit scholarships are awarded based on academic performance. Contact the student services office for available opportunities.

Scholarship options typically include: academic merit awards, need-based grants, sports scholarships, and government-funded programs. Check your university's scholarship portal for eligibility criteria!
To apply for scholarships: maintain a high GPA, submit the scholarship application form, provide supporting documents, and write a strong personal statement. Deadlines vary, so apply early!

patterns: How can I access library resources
What resources does the library have
responses: The university library typically offers: physical books, e-journals, research databases, study rooms, and digital resources. Most libraries are open Monday-Friday 8AM-10PM and weekends 9AM-6PM.
University libraries provide access to: textbooks, research papers, journals (IEEE, Springer, etc.), computers, and quiet study spaces. You can access many resources online with your student ID!
Library resources include physical collections, online databases (JSTOR, PubMed, ScienceDirect), and interlibrary loan services. Visit the library portal with your student credentials!

patterns: Tell me about the exam schedule
responses: Exams are generally held at mid-semester (midterms) and end of semester (finals). Schedules are posted on the university portal. Grading typically uses GPA on a 4.0 scale!
Most universities conduct: quizzes, assignments, midterm exams, and final exams. The typical breakdown is: Quizzes 10%, Assignments 20%, Midterm 30%, Final 40%. Check your course outline!
Passing grades typically require 50-60% overall. GPA is calculated based on grade points (A=4.0, B=3.0, C=2.0, D=1.0, F=0). Maintain at least a 2.0 GPA to remain in good standing!

patterns: How much is the hostel fee
responses: Many universities offer on-campus dormitories/hostels for students! Facilities typically include shared or private rooms, common areas, Wi-Fi, and meal plans. Apply early as spaces are limited!
On-campus accommodation options usually include: single rooms, double rooms, and dormitory-style housing. Off-campus options include nearby apartments. Contact the housing office for availability!
University hostels provide a safe and convenient living option. They usually have room options, cafeteria services, and 24/7 security. Application is done through the student services portal.

patterns: What programming languages should I learn
responses: For beginners, Python is the best first language! It's readable, versatile, and widely used in AI/ML, web development, and data science. After Python, consider JavaScript, Java, or C++!
Top programming languages to learn: Python (AI/data), JavaScript (web), Java (enterprise), C++ (systems), and SQL (databases). Start with Python for the easiest learning curve!
Learning to code: Start with Python basics → practice on LeetCode → build small projects → contribute to open source. Online resources: Coursera, edX, freeCodeCamp, and CS50 are excellent!

patterns: What is a large language model
responses: Machine Learning is a subset of AI where systems learn from data to make predictions without being explicitly programmed. It includes supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning!
AI (Artificial Intelligence) is the broad concept of machines performing smart tasks. ML is a subset using statistical techniques. Deep Learning is a subset of ML using neural networks!
Neural networks are computing systems inspired by biological neurons. They process information through layers of nodes, learning patterns from data. They power modern AI like ChatGPT!
I don't have access to real-time weather data, but you can check weather.com or Google for current weather conditions in your area!
I'm an NLP bot without internet access, so I can't check live weather. Try asking Google 'what's the weather today' for accurate forecasts!
For weather updates, I recommend checking AccuWeather, Weather Underground, or your phone's weather app. I focus on NLP and university questions!
Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs! 😄
Why was the neural network stressed? It had too many layers of anxiety! 🤖
What do you call a sleeping dinosaur? A dino-snore! 😂 (Sorry, I specialize in NLP, not stand-up comedy!)
Why did the NLP model break up with the dataset? It said 'You have too many noisy labels!' 😆

I can't check the current time as I'm a text-based AI. Check your device's clock or ask Google for exact time!
For current time and date, check the clock on your device. I'm more useful for NLP topics and university FAQs!

This is a Smart NLP Chatbot built as a university project! It uses: NLTK for text preprocessing, TF-IDF for feature extraction, and Logistic Regression for intent classification. Frontend uses React.js with Tailwind CSS!
This chatbot was built for an NLP university subject! Technologies used: Python Flask (backend), React.js (frontend), NLTK, TF-IDF vectorizer, and Scikit-learn's Logistic Regression.
This project demonstrates core NLP concepts: tokenization, lemmatization, stopword removal, TF-IDF vectorization, and intent classification. It's a full-stack web application with dark/light mode!
